dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8110 IH: Cyber Ready Workforce Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Lee of Nevada (for herself and Mr. Fitzpatrick) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To establish a grant program within the Department of Labor to support the creation, implementation, and expansion of registered apprenticeship programs in cybersecurity.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Cyber Ready Workforce Act.

paragraph.text: The term registered apprenticeship program means a program registered under the Act of August 16, 1937 (commonly known as the National Apprenticeship Act; 29 U.S.C. 50 et seq.).

The term workforce intermediary means an entity that facilitates the establishment of registered apprenticeship programs, and may be a partnership that includes one or more of the following as partners:

subparagraph.text: A community-based organization, as defined in section 3201(5) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7801(5)).
A State board or local board, as such terms are defined in section 3 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3102).
A postsecondary education institution with experience in developing and administering registered apprenticeship programs.
An institution of higher education, as defined in section 101(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1001(a)).

subsection.text: The Secretary of Labor shall award grants, on a competitive basis, to workforce intermediaries, to support the establishment, implementation, and expansion of registered apprenticeship programs in cybersecurity.

For purposes of subsection (a), registered apprenticeship programs in cybersecurity shall include technical instruction, workplace training, and industry-recognized certification in cybersecurity. Programs shall—

paragraph.text: include certifications in CompTIA Network+, CompTIA A+, CompTIA Security+, Microsoft Windows 10 Technician, Microsoft Certified System Administrator, Certified Network Defender, Certified Ethical Hacker, ISACA Cybersecurity Nexus (CSX), (ISC)2's Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), or other industry-recognized certification in cybersecurity;
encourage stackable and portable credentials; and
lead to occupations such as computer support specialists, cybersecurity support technicians, cloud computing architects, computer programmers, computer systems analysts, or security specialists.

subsection.text: A workforce intermediary shall use at least 85 percent of the amount of grant funds received under this Act for the following:

paragraph.text: Complete the apprenticeship registration process with the Department of Labor, and assist employers with other logistical and technical issues.

subparagraph.text: Develop curricula and technical instruction for the registered apprenticeship program in cooperation with local businesses, organizations, and employer-partners, referencing the work roles and tasks outlined in the National Initiative for Cybersecurity Education (NICE) Cybersecurity Workforce Framework Special Publication 800–181 to develop skills and standards for the program.
Assist employers in paying for the cost of offsite training and acquiring course materials provided to apprentices.
Connect employers with education and training providers to complement on-the-job learning.

paragraph.text: Provide support services to apprentices to assist with their success in the registered apprenticeship program, which may include the following:

subparagraph.text: Assisting with costs of transportation, housing, and child care services.

subsection.text: A workforce intermediary may use up to 15 percent of the amount of grant funds received under this Act for the following outreach and marketing activities:

paragraph.text: Market apprenticeships and the apprenticeship model to employers, secondary school administrators, and counselors.
Recruit and conduct outreach to potential apprentices, including secondary school students, underrepresented populations (such as women and minorities), youth, and veterans.
Connect and collaborate with other workforce intermediaries, and coordinate resources with Federal investments in the registered apprenticeship program, to—

subparagraph.text: widely disseminate training resources and materials developed with grant funds provided under this Act.

section.text: There is authorized to be appropriated, such sums as may be necessary to carry out this Act.
